section.title: SageMath Examples
section.p:
SageMath is a Computer Algebra System, like Maple, MATLAB, and Mathematica. Sage is based on Python and is open source, and free to use.
Here are some SageMath cells where you can do certain things that you might not be able to do with the graphing apps we will use. You can return here to execute SageMath commands. I will add SageMath examples from class here as we go.

paragraphs.title: Solve a Linear System
paragraphs.p: Note the double equals sign for making a comparison. Also note that multiplications must be explicit: like 2*x, not 2x.

paragraphs.title: Row Reduce Matrices
paragraphs.p: The QQ stands for the rational numbers, so these matrices will accept rational numbers as entries. And the row reduction process will work in the usual way, where it is legal to do things like divide by an integer. The echelon_form() command gives reduced row echelon form.

sage.input: A = matrix( QQ, # number field to use [ # the matrix, entered as a nested array [1, 2, 1], [4, 5, 4] ] ) A.echelon_form() # echelon_form() method returns A's RREF
